content_type: enforcement-action photo with agency branding overlay
description: A photograph of an ICE deportation/removal operation that has been branded with agency graphics into a shareable card. The photo, shot on an airport tarmac/apron under a blue sky, shows a young man in a gray t-shirt and dark jacket standing facing the camera, flanked from behind by two officers wearing high-visibility orange safety vests: the left vest reads 'S.A.F.E.' and the right vest reads 'ICE,' with a marker '4' on a wall behind. Overlaid in the lower-left corner is the gray U.S. Department of Homeland Security seal, the white 'ICE' wordmark, a blue 'LEARN MORE' button, and the URL 'ICE.gov/newsroom' in white. The design technique frames a deportation as a triumphant law-enforcement result; the subject (described in the tweet as a Salvadoran fugitive wanted for aggravated homicide) is centered and exposed while officers' faces are turned away. Target audience: the public, signaling ICE's removal of alleged criminal aliens. Tweet text: 'This Salvadoran fugitive - wanted for aggravated homicide and terrorist organization - no longer threatens Americans. We finally removed him.'
